pages.full_text:
does the cooking. Veg garden. Compost heap. Real towel in bathroom. Homemade benches + slide. V nice but oh dear! we were greeted by 2 horse-flies. Masses of flies. V. natural + healthy but fly-ridden.
    Charles has suggested going over the Susten Pass.
    Supper at the campsite restaurant - pizza + chocolate cake. V. full. We are going to use the fly screens tonight.
__Tuesday__ I hand washed some clothes. Simon cleaned the windscreen.
  We set off in the direction we had been cycling. Glaciers. Through the Susten pass. V [?beautiful] but dangerous bends, some straight after tunnels. Mad motorcyclists. After the pass, (a short tunnel) we zigzagged down, going through Dorfi ([?Ibdis] village?) + south through the St. Gotthard Tunnel. 17R long.
[?Later]    On along complicated fast roads, past Lake Lugano + near Como. We went to the Fiamma huge shop that made the awning that broke. They don't have the parts that broke. (probably sold out to other people with busted awnings). 12 other vans in carpark
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence — continuation from previous spread. No handwritten page number visible at top-left. The word after 'V' before 'but dangerous bends' is partially obscured by the gutter; 'beautiful' is the most likely reading but not certain. '[?Ibdis]' is very uncertain — the parenthetical '(Ibdis village?)' may be the diarist's own query about the village name, making this possibly 'Ibdis' or a similar Swiss village near the St. Gotthard. '17R long' likely means 17km long (referring to the St. Gotthard Road Tunnel, which is approximately 17km). The word at the start of the 'On along' paragraph is unclear — possibly 'Later' or a place annotation. 'Fiamma' is an Italian awning manufacturer for campervans. The right-side portion of the spread continues this text. Year estimated as 2016 based on 'Wednesday June 22nd' on the right page (June 22 was a Wednesday in 2016) and context from nearby transcripts.

pages.full_text:
probably all after bloody broken awning parts. Simon thinks he's bought enough bits to mend it.
  V hot + sweaty. On the to Pavia. Yoga, shower, supper, walk.
  We passed poplar plantations and [green] rice paddy fields. Poplars put up with water, so they are good for making pallettes.
  Walter + Austin have both got into schools for the autumn. It will be the first time that all 5 children will be in school. Olive is 7. She will be 8 in November.
__Wednesday June 22nd.__
  Rain, but still warm. In the morning we swam. [The] I used [used] the washing machine. We cycled into Pavia + went to St. Michael Major church then the cathedral - dome work[ed] in by [Leonardo + Bramante?] - impressive marble. We went on to the big square. While we were in the cathedral Charles telephoned. We saw huge square tower (why built?) in the university quarter + finished our visit at St. Tadeo.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '68' visible at top-right. 'Wednesday June 22nd' is underlined. The word 'green' appears to be written above 'rice paddy fields' as an interlinear annotation (the diarist adding 'green' to describe the paddies). 'pallettes' is the diarist's spelling (for 'pallets'). 'Walter + Austin' are family members (children) who got school places. 'Olive is 7. She will be 8 in November' — Olive is named elsewhere in the diary; her being 7 turning 8 in November 2016 is consistent with other entries. The annotation 'dome work[ed] in by Leonardo + Bramante?' is written interlinearly above 'then the cathedral - impressive marble' — the attribution of Pavia Cathedral to Leonardo da Vinci and Donato Bramante is historically noted (they contributed to the design). '[The] I used [used]' appears to be a self-correction in the original — 'The' struck through and 'used' written twice. 'St. Tadeo' likely refers to the church of San Teodoro (St. Theodore) in Pavia. June 22nd fell on a Wednesday in 2016, matching the 'Wednesday June 15th' entry on B_0365_L (likely the same trip, one week earlier).
